sections.paragraph:
(“brother of the king”), [Ahimelech], pr.n.-

(1) a priest living ar Nob, the father of Abiathar, the intimate friend of David ( 1Sa 21:2 20:9 Psa 52:2), and therefore slain by Saul. Different from this apparently may be-(2) Ahimelech, the son of Abiathar, one of the two high priests in the time of David, 2Sa 8:17 1Ch 24:3, 1Ch 24:6, 31 1Ch 24:31. Korb, however (Winer Theol. Journal IV. p. 295), supposes, with a great deal of probability, that in 2Sa 8:17, for “Ahimelech, the son of Abiathar,” we should read, “Abiathar, the son of Ahimelech;” from this erroneous reading he supposes that a mistake was introduced into the Chronicles.
